- Goal model: Move green objects to target positions marked by g frames. Each move reduces the count by 1 (source moves to target, another gets removed).

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The pattern is working consistently:
1. Select green at (19, 30) -> g frame appeared at (19, 42)
2. Click g frame at (19, 42) -> green moved to (19, 42), green at (19, 36) disappeared
3. Now 3 greens remain: (19, 42), (25, 42), (37, 42)
The pattern is clear: each move consolidates the green objects. They're all moving toward the right column (col 42). Let me continue:
- Select (19, 42) -> target will be somewhere
- Click target -> move
- Repeat until done
Let me continue the loop. I'll select the next green and move it.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Pattern confirmed! Select green -> g frame appears at target -> click target -> green moves there, another disappears. Now 3 greens at (19, 42), (25, 42), (37, 42). All in column 42.
Plan: Continue the select-and-move loop until all objects are consolidated or level completes.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# 3 greens remain: (19, 42), (25, 42), (37, 42)
# Continue the loop: select, find g frame, click it

def get_centers(seg, color):
    nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == color]
    centers = []
    for n in nodes:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']), sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']))
        centers.append(center)
    return centers

# Select first remaining green at (19, 42)
print("Selecting green at (19, 42)...")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 42}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
g_centers = get_centers(seg, 'g')
print(f"g frame centers: {g_centers}")

if g_centers:
    # Average to find target
    target_r = sum(c[0] for c in g_centers) // len(g_centers)
    target_c = sum(c[1] for c in g_centers) // len(g_centers)
    print(f"Clicking target at ({target_r}, {target_c})...")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': target_r, 'col': target_c}])
    
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    green_centers = get_centers(seg2, 'N')
    print(f"Greens after: {green_centers}")
    
    # Continue loop
    if len(green_centers) > 1:
        # Select first green
        print(f"\nSelecting green at {green_centers[0]}...")
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': green_centers[0][0], 'col': green_centers[0][1]}])
        
        seg3 = current_frame.segmentation
        g_centers3 = get_centers(seg3, 'g')
        if g_centers3:
            target_r3 = sum(c[0] for c in g_centers3) // len(g_centers3)
            target_c3 = sum(c[1] for c in g_centers3) // len(g_centers3)
            print(f"Clicking target at ({target_r3}, {target_c3})...")
            action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': target_r3, 'col': target_c3}])
            
            seg4 = current_frame.segmentation
            green_centers4 = get_centers(seg4, 'N')
            print(f"Greens after: {green_centers4}")
            print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")
            print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
